options.text: The lungs are often filled with water and require deep suctioning.
Laryngospasm prevents water from entering the lungs.
Abdominal thrusts should always be performed before ventilating the patient.
Manual suction devices are not powerful enough to remove water and fluids from the airway.

explanation: Laryngospasm is a protective reflex where the vocal cords spasm to prevent water entry into the trachea. In most near-drowning incidents, this spasm prevents significant amounts of water from actually entering the lungs, despite the patient appearing to have aspirated.
